message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Bar Issi (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~322 reviews; Tripadvisor ~22 reviews (younger venue, opened June 2025)
- Sentiment: mixed
- Longevity: Opened June 6, 2025 — under 1 year operating. Ownership stable: Marissa and Matt Hermer (Olivetta, Chez Mia in LA); Bar Issi is their first venture outside Los Angeles.
- Credentials: ["The Infatuation 8.0/10 review","Wallpaper* travel review feature","Time Out Palm Springs feature","Sunset Magazine 'Hottest New Restaurant Opening' feature","Palm Springs Life 'New Dining in the Desert' feature","Locale Magazine 'Best New Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs' inclusion"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly in 'best new restaurants Palm Springs', 'Italian restaurant Palm Springs', and 'Thompson Hotel Palm Springs dining' queries. Surfaces in 'date night Palm Springs' coverage. Does NOT consistently surface in broad 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining Palm Springs' agent answers — those still default to established names (Workshop Kitchen + Bar, Copley's, Mamma Gina, Johnny Costa's, Il Corso). Effectively invisible in 'Uptown Design District' fine-dining lists despite the address.
- Site structured data: thebarissi.com presents address, phone, email, reservation link, and menu navigation cleanly. Hours are NOT prominently surfaced on the homepage (only general patterns like 'nightly dinner, brunch Fri-Sun'); third-party sources list more specific hours (M-F 4:30-9:30pm; Sat-Sun 11am-10:30pm) [UNVERIFIED on first-party site]. Schema.org JSON-LD / Restaurant microdata could not be confirmed from rendered content — likely minimal or absent. Hyatt/Thompson parent page provides additional machine-readable signal.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what The Infatuation says about Bar Issi in Palm Springs:

Bar Issi is located in the Thompson Hotel on Palm Canyon and is an over-the-top Italian restaurant that will remind everyone—even locals—that while in Palm Springs, they're on vacation. While a ballroom-sized dining room with pineapple chandeliers, pink alligator wallpaper, and fringed bar stools would be a local eyesore in most cities, in Palm Springs, Bar Issi fits right in.

The restaurant offers bright pink hibiscus cosmos and puffy Neapolitan pizzas decorated with vegetables from the farmers' market, and the al dente pastas, including a standout radiatori in a sweet lamb bolognese, are some of the best in the Valley. For large groups, it's recommended to book a table at least a week out, but for casual dates and drop-bys, you can head to the walk-in bar.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Issi and other new restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Bar Issi

Bar Issi is a breezy and elevated coastal Italian restaurant that opened June 6 in the Thompson Hotel Palm Springs, by Hyatt. It's a Mediterranean eatery that has become a buzzy hotspot with tough-to-nab reservations, featuring a maximalist 180-seat space decked with wild patterns and feathery pink glass chandeliers.

The Bar Issi menu is crafted with seasonal California ingredients and Italian traditions, offering house made pastas, wood-fired pizzas and raw bar favorites in an expansive space serving nightly dinner and brunch Friday through Sunday. The design was inspired by late style icon Iris Apfel, the second season of The White Lotus, the Rat Pack, and filmmaker Wes Anderson.

## Other New Restaurants in Palm Springs 2026

The dining scene in Palm Springs has expanded beyond Bar Issi. Beaton's at Bar Cecil offers expertly mixed cocktails, luxe bar bites, and a glamorous room, with a lively but relaxed vibe ideal for a memorable date night.

Bougainvillea Fresh Cuisine is a small off-the-radar spot in a Warm Sands strip mall getting rave reviews from locals, with a menu drawing from Latin and Mediterranean traditions.

Additional options include Lola Rose, which blends stylish interiors and mountain views with elevated Eastern Mediterranean-comfort food at the Thompson hotel, serving as a tasty and reliable choice for daytime or evening dining.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Issi, Hermer, and Olivetta:

Marissa and Matt Hermer, the hospitality power couple behind hotspots Olivetta and Chez Mia, have officially opened Bar Issi, their newest restaurant and their first venture outside Los Angeles. Bar Issi debuted June 6, 2025, and is now open for brunch, dinner, and drinks inside the Thompson Hotel Palm Springs.

The new concept blends old-money Italian coastal flavors with a free-spirited California sensibility—and a whole lot of style. Bar Issi debuted with a bold, maximalist design by Tom Parker of Fettle, awash in Italian reds, blues, greens, and rich earth tones. The layered, cinematic look was inspired by Season 2 of The White Lotus, set in Sicily, as well as the iconic Madonna Inn in San Luis Obispo, famous for its wild floor-to-ceiling patterns and theatrical design.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent Italian restaurants for a date night in Palm Springs in 2026:

## Top Recommendations

**Il Corso** - Il Corso offers an authentic Italian dining experience with beautiful ambiance and excellent food and service, with lovely atmosphere and impeccable service that make it perfect for a date night. They source many products from Italy and Sicily, including San Marzano tomatoes, authentic Bufala Mozzarella, and Pecorino Romano cheeses, along with an extensive wine list.

**Il Giardino** - This is a hidden gem serving authentic Sicilian and Northern Italian cuisine with house-made pasta and family recipes. The style of dining with multiple courses makes Il Giardino especially appealing to those seeking a more intimate, sit-down dinner experience for a date night. Located at 333 Indian Canyon, just a short walk from downtown Palm Springs, it opened in 2015 and continues to serve customers with delicious Italian/Sicilian cuisine.

**Birba** - Offering alfresco dining with Italian specialties beneath a flowering tree with romantic string lights, this cozy date night spot features a central fire pit for charming ambiance and serves pasta, pizzas, and creative cocktails.

All of these restaurants are well-suited for date nights and offer authentic Italian cuisine in romantic settings. It's recommended to make reservations in advance, especially during peak season.

Based on the search results, I found information about Bar Issi and Sunset Magazine's coverage. Here's what was found:

Bar Issi is a new downtown restaurant in Palm Springs from L.A.-based Boujis Group, the visionary team behind L.A. hotspots Olivetta and Chez Mia. The California-Mediterranean restaurant is located at the new Thompson Hotel and shops on North Palm Canyon Drive.

Sunset Magazine visited the Thompson Hotel to check out what locals had been telling them was the hottest opening in years. Designed by Fettle, Bar Issi is a maximalist space with high gold mohair-lined ceilings, whimsical wallpaper of cavorting alligators, and chic furnishings in jewel tones, creating a high-energy theatrical air that feels like a mashup between the best brasseries of Paris and the restaurant at a grand Italian coastal hotel.

The menu channels Mediterranean and Californian cuisine with offerings like burrata and sun gold tomatoes, marinated lamb chops, blistered pizzas, branzino, gougères, pastas, and caviar service.

- The Steakhouse is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award and has won the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007.
- Morgan's in the Desert, located at La Quinta Resort & Club, offers a refined dining experience focusing on farm-to-table cuisine.
- The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage specializes in prime steaks and fresh seafood with stunning views of the Coachella Valley.

**French & Italian Cuisine:**
- Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.
- Trilussa in Cathedral City is known for its classic Italian cuisine, offering traditional Italian dishes from homemade pasta to veal marsala.

**Seafood:**
- Oceana Restaurant in Palm Desert features fresh seafood with exquisite flavors and stunning ambiance.

**Contemporary American:**
- EIGHT4NINE Restaurant & Lounge in Palm Springs combines modern American cuisine with a chic, contemporary atmosphere, offering everything from classic steaks to innovative vegetarian dishes.

Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week returns May 29 - June 7, 2026, offering residents and visitors 10 days of exquisite dining experiences throughout the nine-city oasis.

Based on the search results, here are the fine dining restaurants in the Uptown Design District of Palm Springs:

## Fine Dining Options

Copley's on Palm Canyon is a chef-owned fine dining restaurant set in a historic estate. Located on the grounds of Cary Grant's former guest house, Copley's was recently named one of the top 100 romantic restaurants in North America by Open Table.

Eight4Nine Restaurant & Lounge is known for its striking modern interior, impeccable service, and beautifully plated contemporary cuisine, with a sleek dining room filled with white tones, bold artwork, and dramatic lighting.

Workshop Kitchen + Bar is a stunning modern space and one of the city's most stylish dining destinations for foodies.

## Other Notable Dining Destinations

The Uptown Design District has become one of the most exciting dining neighborhoods in Palm Springs, stretching along North Palm Canyon Drive with a stylish corridor blending midcentury architecture, boutique hotels, art galleries, and design shops. Within a few walkable blocks, visitors can enjoy everything from chef-driven fine dining and creative sushi to lively brunch spots and relaxed patios perfect for sunset cocktails.

I found relevant search results for your query. Here's what I found:

Bar Issi is located inside the Thompson Hotel and features an Italian beach town aesthetic with pink pineapple chandeliers and bold maximalist décor with a menu rooted in coastal Italian flavors.

The restaurant is featured in Locale Magazine's article on the best new restaurants in Greater Palm Springs that are worth making reservations at. Bar Issi brings a vibrant, maximalist Italian vibe to the destination from restaurateurs Marissa and Matt Hermer of Olivetta and Chez Mia.

The 180-seat space offers nightly dinner and brunch Friday through Sunday, with housemade pastas, wood-fired pizzas and raw bar favorites. The restaurant features handmade pastas, wood-fired pizzas and a raw bar, with dishes like radiatori with lamb Bolognese, roasted mushroom pizza with taleggio, and branzino with preserved lemon, plus a cocktail list featuring spritzes and brunch-friendly pours, with happy hour offering $5 wines, oysters and arancini.

content.text: I have enough to verify the key claims. Let me finalize the assessment.

confirms: Yelp ~322 reviews (confirmed: '421 Photos & 322 Reviews' in May 2026 Yelp snippet)
Ownership: Marissa and Matt Hermer of Olivetta and Chez Mia; first venture outside LA (confirmed across Sunset, OneEleven Living, Dining and Cooking)
Wallpaper* travel review feature (confirmed at wallpaper.com)
Sunset Magazine 'Hottest New Restaurant Opening' feature (confirmed: exact title at sunset.com)
Palm Springs Life 'New Dining in the Desert' feature (confirmed)
Locale Magazine 'Best New Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs' inclusion (confirmed)
Site presents address (414 N Palm Canyon Dr Suite F), phone, email, reservation link cleanly
Hours NOT prominently surfaced on homepage (only general 'nightly dinner, brunch Fri-Sun' pattern; no specific times)
JSON-LD / Schema.org Restaurant microdata appears absent on thebarissi.com (BentoBox builder, no script type='application/ld+json' detected)
Surfaces strongly in 'best new restaurants Palm Springs' and 'Thompson Hotel Palm Springs dining' queries (confirmed via search)

Does NOT surface in broad 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' fine-dining agent answers — defaults still favor Morgan's, Workshop, Copley's, Eight4Nine, etc. (confirmed; Bar Issi absent from top fine-dining roundups)
Effectively invisible in 'Uptown Design District' fine-dining lists (confirmed: Copley's, Eight4Nine, Workshop Kitchen + Bar dominate; Bar Issi not surfaced)
contradicts: Claim: 'Surfaces in date night Palm Springs coverage' — partially refuted. In the 'date night Palm Springs Italian restaurant' query, agent answers default to Il Corso, Il Giardino, and Birba; Bar Issi did not surface in the synthesized result despite being heavily covered editorially. Bar Issi's date-night surfacing is weaker than claimed.

Claim sentiment 'mixed' — Tripadvisor rating of 3.5/5 with rank #218 of 309 suggests sentiment is notably mixed-to-poor on Tripadvisor specifically (worse than 'mixed' implies for a buzzy opening); editorial sentiment is glowing while user-review sentiment skews lower. The single 'mixed' label undersells this divergence.
flagged_as_unverified: Specific hours claimed by third parties (M-F 4:30-9:30pm; Sat-Sun 11am-10:30pm) — not independently re-verified in this pass; first-party site does not surface them
Hyatt/Thompson parent page providing 'additional machine-readable signal' — not directly inspected for JSON-LD in this verification
Yelp star rating / sentiment breakdown — review count confirmed but star-distribution not pulled
Visibility gap score of 6 — subjective composite; broadly consistent with findings (strong editorial, weak in broad fine-dining/neighborhood lists, no schema)
Convertibility tier 'anchor-reference' — taxonomy-specific, not externally verifiable
input.notes: Editorial credentials are unusually well-supported — every named publication (Infatuation, Wallpaper*, Time Out, Sunset, Palm Springs Life, Locale) returned a direct hit with matching framing. Review counts match exactly (Yelp 322, Tripadvisor 22). Structured-data finding is correct: thebarissi.com is a BentoBox site with no detectable JSON-LD/microdata, which is a real and confirmable visibility gap. The two soft contradictions: (1) Tripadvisor 3.5/#218-of-309 is worse than 'mixed' suggests for a heavily-PR'd opening — worth flagging as a sentiment divergence between press and patrons; (2) 'date night' surfacing is weaker than claimed — Bar Issi did not appear in date-night Italian results, which still default to Il Corso/Il Giardino/Birba. Both are nuance refinements rather than core refutations. Overall the assessment holds up well under adversarial check.
